scope: natural anonymous PC-Web report/feedback path probe for targetid-related popup handoff

natural_actions: programmatic click on the first hidden comment report button
right-click the player container and inspect the context menu

takeaways: Comment-area report buttons exist in DOM but stay hidden in the tested anonymous PC-Web path.
Hovering sampled .at-feed cards did not reveal a clickable report entry.
Programmatic click on the first hidden report button still produced no report/feedback popup or iframe.
The player context menu stayed hidden, so the built-in feedback entry did not become a natural path either.
The same hidden-comment-report pattern repeats on the pay_status=15 variety topic page.
Natural anonymous PC-Web attempts still produced no targetid-bearing popup, iframe, or window.open report flow.
The feedback/report infrastructure is loaded, but this probe did not find an exposed natural producer.

cross_page_takeaways: Both representative pages load generic feedback/report infrastructure (`universalReportH5`, `tv-feedback.umd.js`), but the tested anonymous natural path did not expose a targetid-bearing producer.
Comment report buttons are present in DOM on both pages, yet remain hidden after sampled hover and stay inert under programmatic click.
The player context menu contains a feedback item in DOM shape, but the menu itself stayed hidden in the tested path.
This round strengthens the negative side of the closure: consumer mapping is now known from synthetic runtime, but anonymous natural producer evidence is still absent.

what_is_now_confirmed: anonymous PC-Web natural attempts on the tested pages do not currently produce report popup / iframe handoff with targetid
comment report buttons exist but remain hidden and inert in the tested path
generic feedback infrastructure loads independently of a visible targetid producer
what_remains_open: player menu or other lazy natural producer branches that require a different interaction surface
whether commentInfo.targetid/commentid can ever flow into a natural report popup
